subject: Re: Artifacts in tomogram
body:
Hello Chen,

Patches on the sides of a tomogram are usually empty due to rotation of the
tomogram so that the tilt axis is aligned vertically.

For the strange contrast, double check your version of IMOD. Versions prior
to 4.12 are unable to correctly read 16 bit MRC files.

Best,
James



On Tue, Dec 17, 2024 at 6:24 PM 'Chen Sun' via Warp <
warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:

> Hi Alister,
>
> Thank you for your swift reply. The center of the tomogram looks okay. The
> image that I showed is only at the edge of the tomogram. The WarpTools
> reconstructed tomograms don't have missing densities. However, the contrast
> looks weird when I open it in 3dmod as unsigned 16-bit integers. Could it
> because it didn't do Find X-ray outliers? If so, is there a way to call the
> find X-ray outlier function with warpTools? Below is the 3dmod interface
> and the warpTools reconstructed tomogram.
> [image: Screenshot 2024-12-17 at 3.18.23 PM.png][image: Screenshot
> 2024-12-17 at 3.21.06 PM.png]
>
> Best,
> Chen
>
>
> On Tuesday, December 17, 2024 at 9:49:32 AM UTC-8 alist...@gmail.com
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Chen,
>>
>> It looks like your alignment completely failed... tomograms should look
>> as you would expect from any reconstruction program e.g.
>> https://warpem.github.io/warp/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/#tilt-series-reconstruct-tomograms
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Tue, 17 Dec 2024 at 09:42, 'Chen Sun' via Warp <
>> war...@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>> I used warpTools to do frame alignment and then use etomo to reconstruct
>>> the tomograms. However, I noticed artifacts in the reconstructed tomogram.
>>> Patches of density were missing. Has anyone seen this before? What could be
>>> wrong that lead to this artifact? Any suggestions would be appreciated.
>>>
>>> Btw, what is the best way to open tomograms reconstructed with warpTools
>>> to visually exam them?
>>>
>>> [image: Screenshot 2024-12-17 at 9.31.44 AM.png]
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Chen
>>>
